This classification places it within the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sunflower order. intro: Centaurea aetoliae, also known as mountain centaury, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its tall stems and distinctive blue to purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea aetoliae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, mountain centaury is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions, including stress and anxiety disorders.
